learning_outcomes.description: Critically assess the contribution and limitations of media reform movements.
Understand the major theoretical traditions in social movement theory.
Have an in-depth understanding of social movement theories and relate these to examples from around the world.

assessments.task_description:
In Class Test.

Write a 700 word answer to questions related to this scenario.

A major fire has destroyed a prominent nature reserve in your neighbourhood. Members of the community had asked the local council to invest in its upkeep although this had not been followed through. They are now angry and have requested a special meeting between the council and local people. They have invited the media to cover this meeting. They have received support from other communities involved in similar actions. They are keen that other nature reserves in their neighbourhood do not meet with the same fate and have embarked on offline and online initiatives aimed at securing council protection for their nature reserves. 

A set of six questions related to the scenario will be handed out along with the answer sheet.

The Required text Castells Power of Identity is essential background reading for this test.
assessments.submission_guidelines: In Class, In Person, Invigilated Test. Laptops, Mobile phones and other devices are prohibited.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.assessment_title: In Class Review of The Power of Identity
assessments.task_description:
Write a review of any chapter from the required text The Power of Identity. The marking criteria will be based on 1) how well you write the introduction, 2) definition of key concepts from that chapter, 3) discussion of its pros and cons and 4) evidence of a reflective conclusion.

Word Length: 700
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description: Do media reform movements contribute towards social change? Have there been successful media reform movements? Using examples from contemporary media reform movements, discuss the pros and cons of media reform and assess what it will take for the media reform movement to become global in scope. What advances in theory characterise/relate to media reform? What are the similarities and differences between media reform and the reform of Big Tech? Your essay will be assessed for your understanding of key concepts related to media reform, the links between theory and practice, illustrations of practice, critique and evidence of research. It will also be assessed for reflective thinking on the theory and practice of media reform.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
